recap:
- Databricks supports various agent development frameworks, including LangChain and DSPy, each tailored for specific use cases.
- LangChain allows the use of Databricks models as LLMs or embeddings, integrates with Mosaic AI for vector storage, and offers MLflow for experiment tracking and observability.
- The LangChain framework features PySpark DataFrame loader and agents for natural language querying, enabling seamless data integration.
- DSPy focuses on programmatically defining generative AI agents, featuring components like modules for text transformations and signatures for input/output behavior.
- DSPy enhances agent development through automated prompt optimization and performance improvement, utilizing a systematic approach rather than manual prompt engineering.
- OpenAI integration in Databricks facilitates native support for developing agents using OpenAI models, enhancing versatility in AI applications.
